audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for entrusted teaching, soldier/athlete/farmer images, faithful saying, approved worker, gentle correction.

verses.restored: You therefore, my child, be empowered in the grace that is in Messiah Jesus.
And the things you heard from me through many witnesses, entrust these to trustworthy humans who will be sufficient to teach others also.
Share hardship as a beautiful/good soldier of Messiah Jesus.
No one serving as soldier entangles oneself in life affairs, so that one may please the one who enlisted.
And if anyone competes, one is not crowned unless competing lawfully.
The laboring farmer must be first to share in the fruits.
Consider what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
Remember Jesus Messiah, raised from the dead, from seed of David, according to my good news,
in which I suffer hardship even to chains as a wrongdoer; but the word of the Divine is not chained.
Because of this I endure all things for the chosen ones, so that they also may obtain salvation/healing in Messiah Jesus with age-long glory.
Trustworthy is the saying: For if we died with him, we will also live with him;
if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny, he also will deny us;
if we are untrustworthy, he remains trustworthy, for he is not able to deny himself.
Remind them of these things, solemnly charging before the Divine not to fight about words, useful for nothing, for overturning the hearers.
Be eager to present yourself approved to the Divine, a worker unashamed, cutting straight the word of truth.
But avoid profane empty-sounds, for they will advance into more irreverence,
and their word will have pasture like gangrene; among whom are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
who missed the mark concerning the truth, saying the resurrection already happened, and they overturn the trust of some.
Nevertheless the firm foundation of the Divine stands, having this seal: The Lord knew those who are his, and, Let everyone naming the name of the Lord stand away from injustice.
But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay, some into honor and some into dishonor.
Therefore if anyone cleanses oneself from these things, one will be a vessel into honor, sanctified, useful to the Master, prepared for every beautiful/good work.
Flee youthful desires, and pursue righteousness/justice, trust, love, peace with those calling upon the Lord from a pure heart.
But foolish and uneducated disputes refuse, knowing that they generate quarrels.
And a servant of the Lord must not quarrel, but be gentle toward all, skilled in teaching, patient under wrong,
in gentleness correcting those opposing, whether the Divine may give them turning/realignment into knowledge of truth,
and they may return to soberness from the snare of the adversarial one, having been captured by him for his will.

translation_notes: Military and athletic metaphors are retained as discipline images, not aggression toward people.
Cutting straight the word of truth is careful handling, not weaponizing text.
Correction is explicitly gentle and restorative, aimed at realignment into truth.
